message:
feat(bench): add hardware provenance, CI width metrics, and run summaries to confidence ledger

Extends the benchmark budget checker and telemetry-perf spec with richer
confidence evidence:

- scripts/bench_budget.sh: Collect host metadata (OS, arch, CPU model, core
  count) at script start; emit ci_width_ns, relative_ci_width, variance_ns2
  in every confidence entry; add hardware provenance block to each entry;
  emit a summary event at end-of-run with aggregate pass/fail/panic/skip
  totals and confidence hint distributions; add json_escape helper for safe
  string encoding in JSON output
- docs/spec/telemetry-perf.md: Update example confidence ledger entry with
  new fields (ci_width_ns, relative_ci_width, sigma_ns, variance_ns2,
  hardware block); document the new run-level summary event schema

Also syncs beads issue tracker.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1400,7 +1400,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.13","title":"Implement: fit-to-container + DPI scaling + font metrics/zoom","description":"Goal
- Make FrankenTerm feel native in the browser: correct cell geometry, crisp text, and stable layout under resize/zoom.

Scope
- Measure and maintain consistent cell width/height across DPR changes.
- Fit-to-container logic (like xterm fit addon) with debounced resize.
- User-controlled zoom/font size with deterministic mapping to cell metrics.

Testing
- Deterministic tests for geometry math (given font metrics + DPR -> cell size).
- E2E: resize storm in browser emits JSONL with geometry snapshots + frame hashes.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WindyDeer","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:42.953909647Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T03:42:06.927341936Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T03:42:06.927325034Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.13","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:42.953909647Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.13","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:42.953909647Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.13","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:42.953909647Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.13","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:42.953909647Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1039,"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.13","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"WindyDeer progress: implemented deterministic DPR/zoom geometry + fit math in crates/frankenterm-web/src/renderer.rs (new GridGeometry, grid_geometry, fit_grid_to_container; WebGpuRenderer set_scale/current_geometry/fit_to_container) and wired wasm API in crates/frankenterm-web/src/wasm.rs (init zoom option, setScale, setZoom, fitToContainer returning geometry snapshots). Added renderer geometry tests: deterministic fractional DPR, fit bounds, zoom capacity reduction, invalid scale normalization. Validation: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test -p frankenterm-web renderer::tests::; cargo check -p frankenterm-web --target wasm32-unknown-unknown.","created_at":"2026-02-09T03:15:27Z"},{"id":1041,"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.13","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress slice (WindyDeer): upgraded tests/e2e/scripts/test_resize_storm.sh to emit richer resize-storm JSONL with deterministic geometry snapshots + per-resize frame hashes. Added geometry fields (cols/rows/dpr/zoom/cell CSS+pixel metrics) on resize events; frame_capture now includes hash_algo + frame_hash + geometry. Added snapshot capture pipeline in run_storm_pty Python path (PTY_STORM_SNAPSHOT_FILE) that records intermediate hashes at resize points and final settled hash. Fixed pre-existing script execution bugs uncovered during validation: replaced env-based call to shell function run_storm_pty with subshell export invocation, and ensured PTY_OUTPUT is set for run_storm_pty Python runner. Validation: bash -n tests/e2e/scripts/test_resize_storm.sh; cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; UBS on changed file (exit 0). Smoke run with ftui-harness binary confirms JSONL now includes multiple frame_capture entries per run with geometry+hash payloads (artifact checks in that run fail as expected under stress, but logging path is functioning).","created_at":"2026-02-09T03:34:17Z"},{"id":1042,"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.13","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"WindyDeer completion update for `bd-lff4p.2.13`.

What landed
- Deterministic geometry + fit/zoom API work (already recorded in prior note):
  - `crates/frankenterm-web/src/renderer.rs`
  - `crates/frankenterm-web/src/wasm.rs`
- Resize-storm E2E JSONL enrichment:
  - `tests/e2e/scripts/test_resize_storm.sh`
    - `resize` events now include geometry snapshot payloads.
    - `frame_capture` events now include `hash_algo`, `frame_hash`, `checksum`, and geometry.
    - PTY runner now writes intermediate/final frame-hash snapshots via `PTY_STORM_SNAPSHOT_FILE`.
    - fixed pre-existing shell-function invocation bug (`env ... run_storm_pty`) by exporting env in a subshell before calling the function.
    - fixed pre-existing missing `PTY_OUTPUT` propagation into Python runner.
    - ensured final settled snapshot sequence number is strictly increasing (`max(existing_seq)+1`) to avoid duplicate frame-capture seq IDs.
- Browser-facing trace helper path:
  - `crates/frankenterm-web/src/frame_harness.rs`: `GeometrySnapshot`, deterministic `stable_frame_hash`, and `resize_storm_frame_jsonl` helpers + tests.
  - `crates/frankenterm-web/src/wasm.rs`: `snapshotResizeStormFrameJsonl` export + shadow cell tracking to hash current frame state deterministically.

Validation
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo check --all-targets`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test -p frankenterm-web frame_harness::tests::`
- `cargo check -p frankenterm-web --target wasm32-unknown-unknown`
- `bash -n tests/e2e/scripts/test_resize_storm.sh`
- Smoke run:
  - `STORM_PATTERN=burst STORM_COUNT=3 STORM_INTERVAL_MS=20 E2E_HARNESS_BIN=/data/tmp/cargo-target/debug/ftui-harness tests/e2e/scripts/test_resize_storm.sh`
  - confirmed JSONL contains resize geometry snapshots + frame-capture hash/geometry fields.
  - artifact checks still fail under this stress path in my run environment (existing behavior), but logging requirements for geometry+hash evidence are satisfied.
","created_at":"2026-02-09T03:42:03Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.14","title":"Implement: scrollback virtualization + smooth wheel/trackpad scrolling","description":"Goal
- Make scrollback feel fast and modern even with huge histories.

Scope
- Only render the visible viewport + small overscan; never iterate the whole scrollback each frame.
- Smooth wheel/trackpad scrolling with coalescing.
- Optional inertial scrolling for touch devices.

Testing
- Perf harness: 100k-line scrollback stays interactive; frame-time p95 stays within budget.
- JSONL logs include scrollback size, viewport range, and render cost.
","notes":"2026-02-09 MistyHill progress: implemented core scrollback virtualization foundations in frankenterm-core. Added ScrollbackWindow + Scrollback::virtualized_window(scroll_offset_from_bottom, viewport_lines, overscan_lines) and Scrollback::iter_range(...) for bounded range iteration without full-history scans; exported ScrollbackWindow from lib. Added unit tests for clamping/overscan/small-history behavior. Added parser_patch_bench scrollback_virtualization_bench with 100k-line scenario comparing window compute + virtualized iteration vs full-history iteration. Added frankenterm-web frame_harness scrollback_virtualization_frame_jsonl(...) telemetry helper (scrollback size, viewport/render ranges, overscan, render_cost_us) + test. Validation: cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test -p frankenterm-core; cargo test -p frankenterm-web; cargo fmt --check all green; UBS scanned touched files with no critical findings.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"IvoryBear","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:43.253103405Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T04:36:08.147859237Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T04:36:08.147840152Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.14","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:43.253103405Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.14","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:43.253103405Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.14","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:43.253103405Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.14","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:43.253103405Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1052,"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.14","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"IvoryBear: Implemented scrollback viewport virtualization + smooth scroll physics in crates/frankenterm-web/src/scroll.rs (838 lines). Key components: ScrollState (offset/velocity/inertia), WheelCoalescer (per-frame batching), ViewportSnapshot (render range with overscan), ScrollFrameStats (JSONL logging). 32 unit tests including 3 stress tests (100k-line scrollback sweep, 1000-frame continuous scroll, overscan bounds). Added benchmark suite in renderer_bench.rs (viewport_compute, coalesce, full_frame_cycle, inertia_convergence). All quality gates pass (fmt, check, clippy, 96/96 lib tests). Committed in fdd86e07.","created_at":"2026-02-09T04:36:02Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.15","title":"Implement: in-terminal search (scrollback search + highlight + next/prev)","description":"Goal
- Provide xterm.js-class search UX: find text in scrollback, highlight matches, and jump next/prev.

Scope
- Search should respect Unicode normalization and be fast on large scrollback.
- Highlighting must be incremental (avoid full redraw).

Testing
- Unit tests for search correctness on Unicode + wrapped lines.
- Perf tests on large scrollback.
","notes":"Progress 2026-02-09 (continued): added ScrollState::jump_to_line(total_scrollback_lines, viewport_rows, target_line) to support search next/prev navigation by moving viewport to match lines with center-when-possible behavior and clamped edge behavior. Added tests: jump_to_line_in_empty_buffer_is_noop, jump_to_line_near_bottom_keeps_bottom, jump_to_line_places_target_inside_viewport, jump_to_line_near_top_clamps_to_oldest_window. Re-ran gates after this change: cargo fmt --check; CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ftui-target-chartreuse cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test -p frankenterm-web; cargo check -p frankenterm-web --target wasm32-unknown-unknown. Also ran UBS on crates/frankenterm-web (known existing findings; no new blocker from this slice).

Progress 2026-02-09 (wasm API wiring): integrated search state into crates/frankenterm-web/src/wasm.rs. Added wasm exports setSearchQuery/searchNext/searchPrev/clearSearch/searchState with configurable caseSensitive/normalizeUnicode, persistent SearchIndex state, active match tracking, and active-match highlight via existing selection overlay path (while preserving explicit selection precedence). Added internal refresh hooks on resize/fit/applyPatch and deterministic search state JS snapshot fields. Added wasm-unit tests (cfg wasm file) for query indexing, wrap navigation, selection precedence, and buffer-change refresh behavior. Validation: cargo fmt --check; CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ftui-target-chartreuse cargo check -p frankenterm-web --target wasm32-unknown-unknown; CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ftui-target-chartreuse cargo test -p frankenterm-web; CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ftui-target-chartreuse cargo check --all-targets; CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ftui-target-chartreuse cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings. UBS run on crates/frankenterm-web reports pre-existing broad findings (including existing panic in scroll.rs test); no new blocker from this patch.

Progress 2026-02-09 (wrapped lines): extended SearchIndex with build_wrapped(lines: (&str, wrapped)) to support logical-line matching across soft-wrap continuations while keeping hard line breaks as boundaries. Added tests search_build_wrapped_matches_across_soft_wrap_boundary and search_build_wrapped_does_not_cross_hard_line_breaks in crates/frankenterm-web/src/scroll.rs. Re-ran gates: cargo fmt --check; CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ftui-target-chartreuse cargo check -p frankenterm-web --target wasm32-unknown-unknown; CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ftui-target-chartreuse cargo test -p frankenterm-web (123 tests); CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ftui-target-chartreuse cargo check --all-targets; CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ftui-target-chartreuse cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CloudyDeer","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:43.567072847Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T10:04:28.160797809Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.15","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:43.567072847Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.15","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:43.567072847Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.15","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:43.567072847Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.15","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:43.567072847Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-lff4p.2.16","title":"Implement: accessibility layer (screen reader + high-contrast + reduced motion)","description":"Goal
- Ensure FrankenTerm is usable for keyboard-only users and screen readers despite using WebGPU/canvas rendering.

Scope
- DOM mirror mode (offscreen/hidden) for screen readers with ARIA roles.
- High-contrast and reduced-motion support.
- Keyboard navigation and focus indicators.

Testing
- E2E: a11y mode scripts + JSONL logs.
- Static checks for ARIA invariants.
","notes":"2026-02-09 BlackCat progress update: added host-side accessibility APIs and invariant-backed model in frankenterm-web. Implemented focused state tracking, accessibilityState(focused), accessibilityDomSnapshot(), accessibilityClassNames(), bounded announcement queue, and toggle-change announcements. Added internal AccessibilityDomSnapshot model + validate() static ARIA invariant checks, with unit tests for valid snapshot invariants and screen-reader-off value behavior. Additional tests cover noop detection (input.rs), announcement queue bounds, and focus blur handling. Validation: cargo test -p frankenterm-web (95 tests pass), cargo check -p frankenterm-web --target wasm32-unknown-unknown pass, cargo check --all-targets pass. Workspace clippy/fmt currently impacted by concurrent edits in scroll.rs and other files outside this bead.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BlackCat","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:43.869839610Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T04:32:01.170535865Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.16","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:43.869839610Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.16","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:43.869839610Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.16","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:43.869839610Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.16","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:43.869839610Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Scope

Testing

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.17","title":"E2E: browser interaction scripts + detailed JSONL logging (FrankenTerm)","description":"Goal
- Add end-to-end browser interaction scripts that validate correctness AND produce high-signal logs.

Requirements
- Scripts cover: selection+copy, paste, OSC8 link click, resize storms, search, and long scrollback.
- Emit JSONL conforming to shared schema, including:
  - run_id, git_sha, browser, DPR, size
  - input counts + encoded bytes
  - frame hashes, patch sizes, and timing histograms

Acceptance Criteria
- Failures yield minimal repro traces that can be replayed deterministically.
","notes":"Implemented non-overlapping sub-slice: added remote selection+copy and search coverage via new scenarios/scripts; wired both into test_remote_all.sh (7 scenarios total); added deterministic repro hints + artifact paths on failures in new scripts. Validation: bash tests/e2e/scripts/test_remote_selection_copy.sh; bash tests/e2e/scripts/test_remote_search.sh; bash tests/e2e/scripts/test_remote_all.sh; cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AmberHollow","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:44.169687801Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T05:39:02.407970376Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T05:39:02.407951601Z","close_reason":"Completed: remote selection/search scripts + schema-valid JSONL telemetry + deterministic repro traces","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.17","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:44.169687801Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.17","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:44.169687801Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.17","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:44.169687801Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.17","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:44.169687801Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.17","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:44.169687801Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.17","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.5.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:13:26.682856111Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.18","title":"Implement: Unicode/emoji rendering correctness (ZWJ, combining marks, wide chars)","description":"Goal
- Ensure FrankenTerm renders the full modern Unicode surface area with terminal-appropriate semantics.

Scope
- Wide glyphs, emoji sequences (ZWJ), variation selectors, combining marks.
- Fallback strategy when shaping is not available.

Testing
- Golden fixtures (strings -> expected cell layout) reused across core + web.
- Visual checksum tests for representative lines.
","notes":"IvoryHawk taking Unicode/emoji closeout pass: verify cross-crate tests and close parent if acceptance criteria are satisfied by existing child slices.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"IvoryHawk","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:44.474276342Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T16:14:49.788474327Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T16:14:49.788454951Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.18","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:44.474276342Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.18","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:44.474276342Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.18","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:44.474276342Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.18.1","title":"Add frankenterm-web patch_feed tests for wide/combining Unicode mapping","description":"Goal: extend web-path Unicode correctness tests in crates/frankenterm-web/src/patch_feed.rs for wide glyph and combining mark handling, ensuring stable RenderCell conversion and attrs/link-id packing behavior remain deterministic. Validation: cargo test -p frankenterm-web, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo fmt --check.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AmberHollow","created_at":"2026-02-09T06:00:12.910604653Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T06:07:15.532877200Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T06:07:15.532854548Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.18.1","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.18","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T06:00:12.910604653Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

- Decide how we will support Windows after the Unix backend is solid.

Acceptance Criteria
- Clear design notes and a dependency plan.
- Not a blocking item for web or Unix progress.
","notes":"Starting design-note pass for Windows backend strategy; will document recommended architecture + dependency plan without blocking Unix/web tracks.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"VioletSparrow","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:54:59.622545463Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T20:29:31.300737447Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T20:29:31.300718632Z","close_reason":"Completed: added Windows native backend strategy + dependency plan + phased non-blocking rollout in docs/spec/frankenterm-architecture.md section 13.5","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.4.9","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:54:59.622545463Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.5","title":"FrankenTerm and ftui-web QA: golden traces, fuzz, extreme optimization","description":"Scope
- Correctness and performance engineering for the entire FrankenTerm + ftui-web stack (core/web/native/remote).

Deliverables
- Golden systems
  - Versioned trace format + replay engine + checksum gates
  - Renderer-level goldens (frame/pixel hashes) for web
- Test stack
  - Proptest invariants + fuzz targets for parsers/state machines
  - Native PTY E2E scripts with detailed JSONL logging
  - Web/remote E2E scripts with deterministic traces + JSONL
- CI + Perf gates
  - wasm build checks
  - time-bounded fuzz/proptest smoke
  - trace replay gates
  - benchmark/perf regression thresholds (with optional statistical guards)

Operating Principle
- Every optimization is single-lever, measured, and backed by isomorphism proof + golden verification.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.5.1","title":"Design: golden trace format for terminal sessions (native + web)","description":"Goal
- Define a stable, versioned trace format we can use to reproduce bugs and gate regressions across core/web/native/remote.

Format Requirements
- Inputs
  - output byte streams (VT/ANSI) and/or patches
  - input events (keyboard/mouse/touch/IME)
  - resize events
  - tick/time deltas (explicitly injected)
- Checkpoints
  - frame hashes and/or patch hashes
  - optional grid snapshots for debugging
- Metadata
  - run_id, git_sha/build_id, seed
  - size, DPR (web), terminal profile/capabilities, TERM identity (remote)
  - perf counters: timing histograms, patch sizes, bytes in/out

Design Constraints
- Forward-migratable schema (versioned, with strict validation).
- Reducible/minimizable: traces must be amenable to automatic minimization (delta debugging).

Acceptance Criteria
- Trace format is versioned and forward-migratable.
- Can be produced by native E2E scripts, web harnesses, and remote PTY sessions.
- Validator produces actionable errors (line + field context).
","notes":"Drafted canonical trace spec at docs/spec/frankenterm-golden-trace-format.md (JSONL bundle layout, record types, hashing/normalization, payload sidecars, CI validation + minimization strategy).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PinkMill","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:55:10.780434801Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T18:45:08.703034522Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T18:45:08.703008854Z","close_reason":"Completed: docs/spec/frankenterm-golden-trace-format.md","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:55:10.780434801Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:55:10.780434801Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-lff4p.5.10","title":"Alien Artifact: statistical perf regression detection (e-values/Bayes)","description":"Goal
- Reduce perf-gate false positives while catching real regressions using principled statistics.

Alien Artifact Techniques
- Anytime-valid hypothesis testing via e-values/e-processes (optional stopping safe).
- Bayesian performance model (posterior over regression magnitude) + Bayes factors for evidence strength.
- Explicit expected-loss decision rule:
  - define loss matrix for false positive (wasted engineer time) vs false negative (shipping regression)
  - choose thresholds/policies that minimize expected loss under measured noise.

Deliverables
- A confidence report and evidence ledger per perf gate:
  - p-value alternatives (e-values), posterior summaries, and Bayes factors
  - dataset summary (runs, variance, hardware)
  - decision + rationale
- Clear override/rollback policy when noise dominates.

Acceptance Criteria
- Perf gates become robust: materially fewer flaky failures without letting real regressions through.
- Outputs are machine-readable and human-auditable.
","notes":"2026-02-09 SilverHarbor: implemented confidence ledger layer in scripts/bench_budget.sh while keeping hard budgets authoritative. Added per-benchmark JSONL file target/benchmark-results/perf_confidence.jsonl with CI interval, posterior_prob_regression (logistic proxy), one-step e_value, one-sided BF10, expected-loss decision hint, and loss matrix (FP=1,FN=5). Added decision policy + schema docs in docs/spec/telemetry-perf.md. Script now resets perf logs each run to avoid stale append drift and snapshots confidence logs on rerun. Validation: bash -n scripts/bench_budget.sh; ./scripts/bench_budget.sh --check-only --json; cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test -q; cargo test -p ftui-extras --features diagram.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilverHarbor","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:13:19.554603628Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T01:28:22.164551842Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.5.10","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:13:19.554603628Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.5.10","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.5.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:13:19.554603628Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.5.10","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:13:19.554603628Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.5.11","title":"CI: run native PTY E2E suite + validate JSONL","description":"Goal
- Ensure the existing PTY-based E2E suite stays green as we replace Crossterm and evolve the stack.

Scope
- Run `tests/e2e/scripts/run_all.sh` (or a curated subset) in CI.
- Validate JSONL logs using the shared schema/validator.

Acceptance Criteria
- CI produces artifacts: JSONL logs + failing trace excerpts.
- Failures link back to minimal repro scripts.
","notes":"Implemented in `.github/workflows/ci.yml` (`e2e-pty` job):
- PTY step now runs `tests/e2e/scripts/run_all.sh` explicitly (native PTY suite entrypoint).
- Kept strict JSONL validation against `tests/e2e/lib/e2e_jsonl_schema.json` and hash registry.
- Added `Build PTY failure repro bundle` step (`if: always()`) that emits:
  - `minimal_repros.md` (failed test -> minimal repro script path)
  - `*_excerpt.log` snippets around failure markers
  - `*_pty_head.hex` first 512 bytes for failed PTY captures
  - copied per-run `summary.json`
- Added always-on JSONL artifact upload (`e2e-pty-jsonl-${{ matrix.os }}`) and repro-bundle upload (`e2e-pty-failure-bundle-${{ matrix.os }}`).

Validation:
- YAML parse check passed via Ruby `YAML.load_file`.
- Shell syntax check passed for `tests/e2e/scripts/run_all.sh` and `scripts/e2e_test.sh`.
- Local sanity run: `tests/e2e/scripts/run_all.sh --quick` produced summary + JSONL + logs under `/tmp/ftui_e2e_ci_sanity_1`.
- Repro-bundle logic dry-run produced `minimal_repros.md`, excerpts, and PTY hex artifacts; sample mapping resolved `inline_basic` -> `tests/e2e/scripts/test_inline.sh`.

Repo-wide gate context during this session:
- `cargo check --all-targets`: passed.
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`: fails in unrelated `frankenterm-web` borrow-check changes (`E0499` in `crates/frankenterm-web/src/glyph_atlas.rs`).
- `cargo fmt --check`: fails due pre-existing formatting diffs in unrelated `frankenterm-web` files.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PearlForge","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:13:45.956127569Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T20:28:01.901082531Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T20:28:01.901063074Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.5.11","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.4.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:13:45.956127569Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.5.11","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:13:45.956127569Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.5.11","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.5.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:13:45.956127569Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.5.12","title":"CI: run golden trace replay gates (core + web + remote)","description":"Goal
- Turn the golden corpus into a hard regression gate.

Scope
- Replay traces through:
  - frankenterm-core (parser/grid)
  - ftui-web runner (state machine + rendering)
  - remote bridge loop (where feasible)

Acceptance Criteria
- CI fails on checksum mismatches with actionable diffs (frame/event indices).
- Time-bounded execution for PRs; expanded coverage on nightly.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.5.13","title":"Alien Artifact: failing-trace minimizer (delta debugging + VOI)","description":"Goal
- When a golden trace fails (checksum mismatch), automatically minimize it to the smallest repro trace that still fails.

Why (User Value)
- Makes regressions debuggable in minutes: a minimized trace is actionable and can be attached to issues/PRs.

Techniques (Alien Artifact)
- Start with classic delta debugging (ddmin) on the event stream.
- Upgrade with Value-of-Information (VOI) heuristics to pick the next reduction step that maximizes expected information gain per replay cost.
- Maintain an evidence ledger: each reduction attempt logs (candidate size, replay time, pass/fail, inferred minimality bounds).

Deliverables
- CLI/API to minimize a trace given a predicate ("replay mismatches").
- Produces a new trace file + a minimization report (JSON/JSONL).

Acceptance Criteria
- On a representative failing trace, produces >=10x size reduction while preserving the failure.
- Deterministic: repeated minimization yields identical minimized trace on same build.
- Integrates into CI failure artifacts (minimized trace attached when feasible/time-bounded).
","notes":"Implemented minimizer in ftui-harness: ddmin + VOI ordering, failure-class-preserving predicate, JSON/JSONL evidence ledger, and env-driven harness entrypoint. Added deterministic + reduction tests in trace_replay. Local validation green: cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test -p ftui-harness trace_replay -- --nocapture. Remaining decision: whether to wire CI artifact publication in this bead or split follow-up.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BlueHollow","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:24:10.918614366Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T21:41:57.953836625Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T21:41:57.953817770Z","close_reason":"Implemented ddmin+VOI failing-trace minimizer API/entrypoint with JSON/JSONL reports; deterministic and >=10x reduction validated in tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.5.13","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:24:10.918614366Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.5.13","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:24:10.918614366Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.5.13","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:24:10.918614366Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 }
 ```

@@ -146,6 +146,21 @@ PANIC_MULTIPLIER=2
 LOSS_FALSE_POSITIVE=1
 LOSS_FALSE_NEGATIVE=5
 
+# Host metadata for confidence ledger provenance.
+HOST_OS="$(uname -s 2>/dev/null || echo unknown)"
+HOST_ARCH="$(uname -m 2>/dev/null || echo unknown)"
+HOST_CPU_MODEL="$(
+    awk -F: '/^model name[[:space:]]*:/ {sub(/^[[:space:]]+/, "", $2); print $2; exit}' /proc/cpuinfo 2>/dev/null ||
+        sysctl -n machdep.cpu.brand_string 2>/dev/null ||
+        echo unknown
+)"
+HOST_CORES_RAW="$(getconf _NPROCESSORS_ONLN 2>/dev/null || nproc 2>/dev/null || echo "")"
+if [[ "$HOST_CORES_RAW" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
+    HOST_CPU_CORES="$HOST_CORES_RAW"
+else
+    HOST_CPU_CORES="null"
+fi
+
 # Colors for output
 RED='\033[0;31m'
 GREEN='\033[0;32m'

+}

 }
 
 run_benchmarks() {
